name: Mahabodhi Temple
description: UNESCO site at Bodh Gaya where the Buddha is said to have attained enlightenment under the Bodhi tree.

name: Shwedagon Pagoda
description: Iconic golden stupa in Yangon, one of the most sacred Buddhist sites in Myanmar.
credit: Photo by Travel Burma (Wikimedia Commons, modified)

name: Temple of the Tooth
description: Temple in Kandy housing a relic of the tooth of the Buddha.
credit: Photo by Miyuru Sankalpa (Wikimedia Commons, modified)

name: Borobudur
description: Massive 9th-century Mahayana temple in Central Java.

name: Wat Phra Kaew
description: Temple of the Emerald Buddha within the Grand Palace complex in Bangkok.

name: Todai-ji
description: Great Buddha Hall in Nara, home to one of the largest bronze Buddha statues.
credit: Photo by Felix Filnkössl (Wikimedia Commons, modified)

name: Haeinsa
description: Temple preserving the Tripitaka Koreana woodblocks.
credit: Photo by Arian Zwegers (Wikimedia Commons, modified)

name: Dhammagiri
description: Vipassana meditation centre associated with the teachings of S. N. Goenka.
credit: Photo by Rohit Sharma (Wikimedia Commons, modified)

name: Sanchi Great Stupa
description: Ancient hemispherical stupa with famous carved gateways; one of the oldest stone structures in India and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Dhamek Stupa
description: Massive cylindrical stupa at Sarnath marking the Deer Park where the Buddha gave his first sermon on the Four Noble Truths.
credit: Photo by R. M. Calamar (via Flickr / Wikimedia Commons, modified)

name: Parinirvana Temple
description: Temple enshrining a long reclining Buddha statue at the place traditionally identified as the Buddha’s final passing into parinirvana.

name: Maya Devi Temple
description: Sacred garden and temple at Lumbini surrounding the marker of the Buddha’s birth, with ancient Ashokan evidence nearby.
credit: Photo by Prince Roy (via Flickr / Wikimedia Commons, modified)

name: Boudhanath Stupa
description: One of the largest spherical stupas in the world and a major center of Tibetan Buddhist practice in the Kathmandu Valley.
credit: Photo by Gita Dhoj Karki (Wikimedia Commons, modified)

name: Swayambhunath
description: Ancient hilltop stupa complex overlooking Kathmandu, revered by both Buddhists and Hindus and nicknamed the Monkey Temple.

name: Ruwanwelisaya
description: Colossal dagoba built in Anuradhapura that remains one of the most revered monuments of Sinhalese Buddhist civilization.
address: Anuradhapura, North Central Province, Sri Lanka

name: Jetavanaramaya
description: Once among the tallest brick structures in the ancient world, this stupa in Anuradhapura marks a major monastic center of Theravāda Buddhism.
address: Anuradhapura, North Central Province, Sri Lanka

name: Gal Vihara
description: Rock-cut Buddha images from the Polonnaruwa period, celebrated masterpieces of Sinhalese Buddhist sculpture.
address: Polonnaruwa, North Central Province, Sri Lanka

name: Dambulla Cave Temple
description: Cave monastery with extensive murals and hundreds of Buddha statues, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Kelaniya Raja Maha Vihara
description: Historic riverside temple near Colombo, linked in tradition to the Buddha’s visits and a major festival site.

name: Mihintale
description: Hill complex regarded as the place where Mahinda introduced Buddhism to Sri Lanka, with ancient steps and ruins.

name: Wat Pho
description: Bangkok temple famous for its enormous reclining Buddha and long-standing role in Thai medicine and monastic education.

name: Wat Arun
description: Iconic riverside prang temple on the Chao Phraya, named for the dawn and central to Bangkok’s Buddhist landscape.

name: Wat Phra That Doi Suthep
description: Hilltop temple near Chiang Mai housing a revered relic and offering sweeping views over northern Thailand.
address: Mueang Chiang Mai District, Chiang Mai, Thailand

name: Wat Mahathat (Ayutthaya)
description: Ruined Ayutthaya monastery known for its stone Buddha head embraced by tree roots, part of a UNESCO historic city.
address: Phra Nakhon Si Ayutthaya, Ayutthaya, Thailand

name: Wat Rong Khun
description: Contemporary White Temple in Chiang Rai blending traditional Buddhist themes with modern symbolic art.
address: Pa O Don Chai, Mueang Chiang Rai District, Chiang Rai, Thailand

name: Kyaiktiyo Pagoda
description: Golden Rock shrine precariously balanced on a cliff, one of Myanmar’s most dramatic pilgrimage destinations.

name: Mahamuni Buddha Temple
description: Mandalay temple housing a highly venerated Buddha image covered in gold leaf by generations of devotees.

name: Shwezigon Pagoda
description: Gold-leafed prototype pagoda of Bagan style, enshrining relics and anchoring the ancient city’s Buddhist landscape.

name: Ananda Temple
description: Elegant Bagan temple with four standing Buddha images, considered a masterpiece of Burmese Buddhist architecture.

name: Bulguksa
description: UNESCO-listed Silla temple near Gyeongju, pairing refined wooden halls with stone bridges and stairways on wooded slopes.

name: Seokguram Grotto
description: Artificial stone grotto on Mount Toham housing a sublime granite Buddha, a pinnacle of Korean Buddhist sculpture.

name: Jogyesa
description: Head temple of the Jogye Order of Korean Buddhism in central Seoul, hub of major ceremonies and urban practice.

name: Tongdosa
description: Major temple near Yangsan traditionally preserving relics of the Buddha and emphasizing the living presence of the Three Jewels.

name: Kiyomizu-dera
description: Timber terrace temple in Kyoto overlooking the city, dedicated to Kannon and a UNESCO World Heritage monument.

name: Senso-ji
description: Tokyo’s oldest temple, centered on Kannon and approached through the bustling Nakamise shopping street.

name: Byodo-in
description: Heian-era Phoenix Hall temple in Uji, famed for Amida Buddha and its reflection in the front pond.

name: Kongōbu-ji
description: Head temple of Shingon Buddhism on Mount Kōya, heart of a vast monastic cemetery and pilgrimage network.
address: Koya, Ito District, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan

name: Enryaku-ji
description: Founding temple complex of Tendai Buddhism on Mount Hiei, overlooking Kyoto and Lake Biwa.

name: Mogao Caves
description: Hundreds of decorated cave shrines preserving Buddhist murals and manuscripts along the Silk Road near Dunhuang.

name: Longmen Grottoes
description: Limestone cliff niches near Luoyang with thousands of Buddhist statues carved from Northern Wei through Tang times.

name: Yungang Grottoes
description: Early Northern Wei cave temples near Datong with colossal seated Buddhas carved from living rock.

name: Leshan Giant Buddha
description: Massive cliff-carved Maitreya figure at the confluence of rivers, among the world’s largest stone Buddha images.

name: Mount Wutai
description: Sacred mountain range with dozens of monasteries, revered in Chinese Buddhism as the abode of Mañjuśrī.

name: Shaolin Monastery
description: Famous Chan Buddhist monastery on Mount Song, historically linked to martial arts and imperial patronage.

name: Lingyin Temple
description: Major Hangzhou temple beside the West Lake hills, with rock-carved Buddhas and busy pilgrimage traffic.

name: Yonghe Temple (Lama Temple)
description: Imperial lamasery in Beijing blending Han and Tibetan Buddhist architecture and large sandalwood Maitreya statue.

name: Famen Temple
description: Ancient Baoji-area temple associated with veneration of the Buddha’s finger bone relic and modern museum complex.

name: Potala Palace
description: Fortress-palace and winter seat of Dalai Lamas in Lhasa, storing chapels, tombs, and vast Buddhist collections.
address: Chengguan District, Lhasa, Tibet Autonomous Region, China

name: Jokhang Temple
description: Holiest temple in Tibet, housing the Jowo Shakyamuni statue and the focus of the Barkhor pilgrimage circuit.
address: Chengguan District, Lhasa, Tibet Autonomous Region, China

name: Sera Monastery
description: One of Lhasa’s great Gelug monasteries, known for structured debate training among monks in the courtyard.
address: Chengguan District, Lhasa, Tibet Autonomous Region, China

name: Drepung Monastery
description: Formerly one of the world’s largest monasteries, a major Gelug seat on the outskirts of Lhasa.
address: Chengguan District, Lhasa, Tibet Autonomous Region, China

name: Tashilhunpo Monastery
description: Traditional seat of the Panchen Lamas in Shigatse, with large chapels and a giant future Buddha statue.
address: Samzhubzê District, Shigatse, Tibet Autonomous Region, China

name: Nalanda Mahavihara (ruins)
description: Archaeological remains of a vast ancient monastic university that attracted scholars from across Asia until the 13th century.

name: Vikramshila Ancient University
description: Ruins of a major Pala-era monastic university on the Ganges plain, historically famed for logic and tantric learning.

name: Ajanta Caves
description: Rock-cut Buddhist caves with narrative murals spanning centuries, a UNESCO masterpiece of Indian art.

name: Ellora Caves (Buddhist)
description: Cliff complex including early Mahayana shrines such as Cave 12 (Teen Tal), part of a UNESCO site shared with Hindu and Jain caves.

name: Kanheri Caves
description: Buddhist rock-cut viharas and chaityas within Sanjay Gandhi National Park, linked to ancient trade with the western Deccan.

name: Hemis Monastery
description: Largest monastery in Ladakh and a major seat of the Drukpa Kagyu lineage, famous for its annual festival.

name: Thiksey Monastery
description: Twelve-story hill monastery in Ladakh resembling the Potala, with a large Maitreya temple and sweeping Indus views.

name: Rumtek Monastery
description: Seat of the Karma Kagyu lineage in exile near Gangtok, modeled on Tibet’s Tsurphu with ornate assembly halls.

name: Tawang Monastery
description: Large Gelug monastery in the eastern Himalayas, founded in the 17th century and culturally tied to Tibetan Buddhism.

name: Gridhakuta (Vulture’s Peak)
description: Hill near Rajgir where the Buddha is said to have taught the Heart Sutra and other Mahayana discourses.

name: Vaishali Ashoka Pillar
description: Site with a lion capital pillar commemorating the Buddha’s last donation bowl and Ashoka’s patronage.

name: Amaravati Stupa (museum site)
description: Important Andhra Buddhist center whose carved limestone reliefs are among the finest early Mahayana sculptural cycles.
address: Amaravati, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh, India

name: Lalitgiri Buddhist Complex
description: Hilltop Odisha site with stupas and monasteries yielding Mahayana sculptures and reliquaries from early centuries CE.

name: Kopan Monastery
description: Tibetan Buddhist monastery above Kathmandu, widely known for teaching Buddhism to international visitors.

name: Pha That Luang
description: National symbol of Laos: a gilded stupa in Vientiane said to enshrine a breastbone relic of the Buddha.

name: Wat Xieng Thong
description: Royal monastery in Luang Prabang with elaborate mosaic facades, a UNESCO-listed town’s spiritual heart.
address: Luang Prabang, Luang Prabang Province, Laos

name: Angkor Wat
description: Vast Khmer temple-mountain later dedicated as a Buddhist site, the centerpiece of the Angkor World Heritage landscape.
address: Siem Reap, Siem Reap Province, Cambodia

name: Bayon
description: Angkor Thom temple famous for serene stone faces, embodying Mahayana Buddhist iconography at the Khmer capital.
address: Angkor Thom, Siem Reap Province, Cambodia

name: Ta Prohm
description: Jungle-shrouded monastery temple at Angkor, left partly unrestored to show nature and monument intertwined.
address: Siem Reap, Siem Reap Province, Cambodia

name: Paro Taktsang (Tiger’s Nest)
description: Cliffside temple complex where Guru Rinpoche is said to have meditated; Bhutan’s most iconic pilgrimage hike.

name: Punakha Dzong
description: Fortress-monastery at the confluence of two rivers, winter seat of Bhutan’s chief abbot and coronation site.

name: Perfume Pagoda
description: Complex of shrines in karst hills southwest of Hanoi, reached by boat and climb, busy during spring festivals.

name: Thien Mu Pagoda
description: Seven-story pagoda on the Perfume River in Hue, symbol of the city and Nguyen-era Buddhist patronage.
address: Huong Long Ward, Hue, Thua Thien Hue, Vietnam

name: Gandan Monastery
description: Active monastery in Ulaanbaatar reviving Mongolian Buddhism after suppression, with large Migjid Janraisig statue.

name: Fo Guang Shan Monastery
description: Large Humanistic Buddhist monastery complex near Kaohsiung with museums, pagodas, and international outreach.

name: Longshan Temple
description: Historic Taipei temple blending Buddhist, Daoist, and folk devotion, centered on Guanyin and local protectors.

name: Buddha Tooth Relic Temple
description: Chinese Buddhist temple in Chinatown built to house a reputed tooth relic of the Buddha in a stupa chamber.

name: Kong Meng San Phor Kark See Monastery
description: Large modern monastery complex in northern Singapore known for education, retreats, and the giant bronze Buddha.

name: Candi Sewu
description: 8th–9th century Mahayana temple compound near Prambanan, one of Java’s major ancient Buddhist ensembles.
address: Bokoharjo, Sleman, Special Region of Yogyakarta, Indonesia

name: Mendut Temple
description: Central Java temple aligned with Borobudur, housing large early Buddhist statues on the pilgrimage axis.

name: Somapura Mahavihara
description: Ruins of a vast Pala-era monastery at Paharpur, a cruciform mahavihara and UNESCO World Heritage Site.
address: Paharpur, Naogaon District, Rajshahi Division, Bangladesh

name: Takht-i-Bahi
description: Well-preserved Gandharan Buddhist monastery on a hill spur, illustrating monastic planning from the Kushan era.

name: City of Ten Thousand Buddhas
description: Large Chan monastery and community in Mendocino County founded by Ven. Hsuan Hua with many halls and statues.
address: 4951 Bodhi Way, Talmage, CA 95481, United States

name: Zen Mountain Monastery
description: Zen training monastery in the Catskills affiliated with the Mountains and Rivers Order and Dharma communications.

name: Nan Tien Temple
description: Southern Hemisphere’s largest Buddhist temple complex, Fo Guang Shan branch south of Sydney.
address: Pioneer Road, Berkeley, Wollongong, NSW 2506, Australia

name: Brickfields Buddhist Maha Vihara
description: Historic Theravāda temple in Kuala Lumpur serving Sri Lankan and Malaysian Buddhists since the late 19th century.
address: 123 Jalan Berhala, Brickfields, 50470 Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

name: Thuparamaya
description: First dagoba built in Sri Lanka after the introduction of Buddhism, holding relics of the Buddha’s hair and collarbone.
address: Anuradhapura, North Central Province, Sri Lanka

name: Shitenno-ji
description: Traditionally counted among Japan’s oldest Buddhist temples, dedicated to the Four Heavenly Kings near Osaka.

name: Baoguo Temple (Mount Emei)
description: Ancient temple on Mount Emei, one of China’s four sacred Buddhist mountains and a UNESCO World Heritage landscape.

name: Mount Jiuhua (Zhiyuan Temple area)
description: Sacred mountain in Anhui associated with Kṣitigarbha bodhisattva, dotted with monasteries and pilgrimage paths.

name: Phimai Historical Park
description: Khmer-era Mahayana Buddhist sanctuary in northeast Thailand, among the best-preserved Angkorian-style monuments in the country.

name: Kek Lok Si Temple
description: Hilltop temple complex on Penang Island with a towering statue of Guanyin and extensive prayer halls.
address: Air Itam, George Town, Penang, Malaysia

name: Tsurphu Monastery
description: Traditional seat of the Karma Kagyu lineage in the Tolung Valley, a major center of Tibetan Buddhist practice.
address: Doilungdeqen District, Lhasa, Tibet Autonomous Region, China

name: Diskit Monastery
description: Nubra Valley monastery in Ladakh with a tall Maitreya statue overlooking the Shyok river basin.

name: Asura Cave (Pharping)
description: Sacred meditation cave south of Kathmandu linked to Padmasambhava and still used by Tibetan Buddhist practitioners.

name: Hsi Lai Temple
description: North American branch monastery of Fo Guang Shan in Greater Los Angeles, with museum, gardens, and large assembly halls.
address: 3456 Glenmark Drive, Hacienda Heights, CA 91745, United States

name: Wat Phra Singh
description: Historic Lanna temple in Chiang Mai housing the revered Phra Singh Buddha image and classic northern Thai architecture.
address: Mueang Chiang Mai District, Chiang Mai, Thailand

name: Chion-in
description: Head temple of Jōdo-shū Pure Land Buddhism in Kyoto, with massive Sanmon gate and vast wooden halls.

name: Shanti Stupa (Rajgir)
description: Japanese-built peace pagoda on Ratnagiri hill near Rajgir, overlooking the valley associated with early Buddhist councils.
